words.definition: Respectful behavior towards someone else.

words.definition: Not having enough of something important.

words.definition: A situation where something becomes worse or is damaged.

words.definition: One who disobeys or breaks rules or laws.

words.definition: A leader who tries to win people over using strong feelings.

words.definition: The social, non-verbal behaviours (such as body language and facial expressions) that are characteristic of a person.

words.definition: The study of people and how they change over time.

words.definition: To make known in a formal manner; to proclaim; to announce; to declare.

words.definition: To show or describe something in a certain way.

words.definition: Deployment

words.definition: Property abandoned by its former owner, especially a ship abandoned at sea.

words.definition: Words that are disrespectful or hurtful.

words.definition: To deprive of inhabitants.

words.definition: Someone or something that is very bad or wicked.

words.definition: Not having what is needed, like food or shelter.

words.definition: Causing damage or harm.

words.definition: A person with deviant behaviour; a deviant, degenerate or pervert.

words.definition: Being very skillful with hands.

words.definition: A division into two different parts.

words.definition: Feeling shy or unsure about oneself.

words.definition: Someone who enjoys something casually but is not serious about it.

words.definition: To make something smaller or less important.

words.sentence: The kids use diplomacy to solve their playground disagreements peacefully.

words.definition: To detect with the senses, especially with the eyes.

words.definition: The quality of being discreet or circumspect.

words.definition: To take something apart piece by piece.

words.definition: To talk about someone or something in a way that shows disrespect.

words.definition: A difference that shows inequality.

words.definition: To sow and scatter principles, ideas, opinions, etc, or concrete things, for growth and propagation, like seeds.

words.definition: To disagree with an idea or opinion.

words.definition: The termination of an organized body or legislative assembly, especially a formal dismissal.

words.definition: To recognize someone or something as different from others based on its characteristics.

words.definition: An activity that helps to take attention away from something else.

words.definition: Easy to teach or control.

words.definition: Having strong opinions that are hard to change.

words.definition: A crossbeam or joist.

words.definition: Very severe or strict.

words.definition: (of a statement) Arousing doubt; questionable; open to suspicion.

words.definition: Being dishonest or saying two different things.

words.definition: One who does not behave like others.

words.definition: A large and impressive building.

words.definition: How well something works.

words.definition: Water that flows out of a place.

words.definition: Believing everyone should be treated equally.

words.definition: To develop in detail or complexity

words.definition: To evoke, educe (emotions, feelings, responses, etc.); to generate, obtain, or provoke as a response or answer.

words.definition: Evading capture, comprehension or remembrance.

words.definition: To set free from the power of another; to liberate; as:

words.definition: An order by the government prohibiting ships from leaving port.

words.definition: To make more beautiful and attractive; to decorate.

words.definition: To steal money that you are supposed to keep safe.

words.definition: Based on real experiences and observations.

words.definition: To try to be like someone or something.

words.definition: To express something in a clear and concise way.

words.definition: To include many different things.

words.definition: To try really hard to do something.

words.definition: Something that is mysterious or hard to understand.

words.definition: A balanced state where everything is equal.

words.definition: Anything that is virtually equal to something else, or has the same value, force, etc.

words.definition: To pull up by the roots; to uproot.

words.definition: A rock moved from one location to another, usually by a glacier.

words.definition: Containing an error; inaccurate.

words.definition: To stay away from something or avoid it.

words.definition: An esoteric doctrine or treatise; esoteric philosophy.

words.definition: Secretly finding out important information.

words.definition: Pertaining to the hypothetical upper, purer air, or to the higher regions beyond the earth or beyond the atmosphere; celestial; otherworldly.

words.definition: Very specific, clear, or detailed.

words.definition: Fop, dandy.

words.definition: To free, disengage, loosen, or untangle.

words.definition: To form into a whole by uniting its parts; to construct; to build.

words.definition: Treating serious issues with (often deliberately) inappropriate humour; flippant.

words.definition: A mistaken belief or idea that is not true.

words.definition: A big failure that is unexpected or embarrassing.

words.definition: Something very obvious that is wrong or bad.

words.definition: Bright and showy in style or appearance.

words.definition: A motion like that of waves; a moving in this and that direction.

words.definition: Staying calm and patient when things are tough.

words.definition: Related to solving crimes using science.

words.definition: Causing fear, dread, awe, or discouragement as a result of size, strength, or some other impressive feature; commanding respect; causing wonder or astonishment.

words.definition: The right to vote at a public election or referendum; see: suffrage, suffragette.

words.definition: Silly or not serious at a bad time.

words.definition: Having no stain or blemish; spotless, undefiled, clear, clean, pure.

words.definition: Unaffected or unable to be affected by something.

words.definition: Not showing due respect; impertinent; bold-faced.

words.definition: Something that gives off light when heated.

words.definition: To stir up or excite; to rouse or goad into action.

words.definition: A physical tilt or bend.

words.definition: Not clear or hard to understand.

words.definition: Two things that do not work well together.

words.definition: Not leading to a clear result or decision.

words.definition: Not easily seen or noticed.

words.definition: A small increase or addition.

words.definition: To accuse or bring criminal charges against.

words.definition: A formal charge of a crime.

words.definition: People or things that come from a specific place.

words.definition: Feeling angry about something unfair.

words.definition: Without care or making distinctions, thoughtless.

words.definition: A thing that is not dispensable; a necessity.

words.definition: To lead by persuasion or influence; incite or prevail upon.

words.definition: One who is not eligible.

words.definition: Not skilled or clumsy at something.

words.definition: Unfairness or lack of justice.

words.definition: The tendency to stay still or keep moving.

words.definition: Without fault or weakness; incapable of error or fallacy.

words.definition: To have a strong but silly love or interest.

words.definition: Any undesirable substance or group of cells that has made its way into part of the body.

words.definition: A non-zero quantity whose magnitude is smaller than any positive number (by definition it is not a real number).
words.sentence: The ant is so small that it seems infinitesimal to us.

words.definition: Any material that causes inflammation

words.definition: (systems theory) An underlying base or foundation especially for an organization or system.

words.definition: Displaying genius or brilliance; tending to invent.

words.definition: The ability to solve difficult problems, often in original, clever, and inventive ways.
words.sentence: Her ingenuity helped us build a cool fort out of blankets and pillows.

words.definition: To try to please someone to gain their favor.

words.definition: Something that is a natural part of something else.

words.definition: To hold back or stop something from happening.

words.definition: A serious unfairness or wrongdoing.

words.definition: Something you are born with or naturally have.

words.definition: Harmless or not likely to offend.

words.definition: Too many to count.

words.definition: Never satisfied or always wanting more.

words.definition: Producing harm in a stealthy, often gradual, manner.

words.definition: To hint; to suggest tacitly (usually something bad) while avoiding a direct statement.

words.definition: The condition of being insolvent; the state or condition of a person who is insolvent; the condition of one who is unable to pay his debts as they fall due, or in the usual course of trade and business.
words.sentence: The company faces insolvency and needs help to stay open.

words.definition: To incite; to bring about by urging or encouraging

words.definition: Very hard to deal with or endure.

words.definition: A person who fights against the local government.

words.definition: Something you cannot touch or see, like feelings.

words.definition: A number, the limit of the sums computed in a process in which the domain of a function is divided into small subsets and a possibly nominal value of the function on each subset is multiplied by the measure of that subset, all these products then being summed.
words.sentence: Eating fruits and vegetables is integral for staying healthy and strong.

words.definition: To speak for someone else to help them.

words.definition: An intermittent fever or disease.

words.definition: To introduce (something) between other things; especially to insert (possibly spurious) words into a text.
words.sentence: We can interpolate the missing numbers in our math puzzle together.

words.definition: To mix two things irregularly, placing things of one kind among things of other:

words.definition: Being alone and enjoying your own company.

words.definition: Lacking freshness, motion, or flow; decaying through stillness.

words.definition: Staying strong and not changing your mind.
